programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: A woman who was injured in the deadly parade crash, Nicole White, testifies.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: Jurors heard about the children and teens affected in the deadly parade crash. The State brought in a high school band director who was leading her students right before the SUV came into the crowd.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: Darrell Brooks wasn't done with the cross-examination of a high school band director who was on the stand. At the beginning of her marching band's set, the red SUV slammed into the crowd and hit a few of her students.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: Darrell Brooks continued the cross-examination of the witness on the stand like he did with the others - with questions that were considered vague, irrelevant and, at times, argumentative.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: An annual family holiday tradition quickly turned into a deadly nightmare. And now the man accused of ramming an SUV into a Christmas parade was standing trial and facing some of those who were there.

programme.title: Deadly Parade Crash Trial
programme.desc: Prosecutors kept bringing in witnesses who had been at the parade when an SUV came crashing into the crowd. The State's latest witness was a woman who had been hit, along with her 9-year-old daughter.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.sub-title: The Case of the Wrong Apartment
programme.desc: Jurors listen to the testimony of two neighbors from the same apartment complex where Jean lived.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: Amber Guyger's defense cross-examines a neighbor; a Texas Ranger testifies about the locks.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: A Texas Ranger provides testimony on how Amber Guyger managed to walk into Botham Jean's apartment.

programme.title: Wrong Apartment Murder Trial
programme.desc: The Texas Ranger investigating this case elaborates on key details about Southside flats.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.sub-title: Secret Life Of Mistresses And Affairs
programme.desc: Yazeed Essa's lifestyle was the focus of testimony from his brother who was on the stand.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: The Defense brought in the background of Yazeed Essa's marriage to Rosemarie. On the stand was his younger brother, Firas. They were close and had been in business together in the years leading up to Rosemarie's death. Firas claims he was out of the country when Rosemarie died. Yazeed's defense team stressed the defendant's emotional state following his wife's death. But the jury would later decide - was he a grieving husband or a guilty murderer?

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: The long testimony of Yazeed Essa's younger brother was not over yet. Firas told the jury about Yazeed's emotional state and demeanor after his wife, Rosemarie, died, the lead-up to her funeral and the police's initial investigation. Firas even claimed that it was his idea to have Yazeed flee the country after he was questioned by police. The Defense tried to paint Yazeed as a grieving husband who panicked and fled the country when his secret life was going to be exposed.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Jurors listened to the man who was accused of helping Yazeed Essa when he was a fugitive. That was his own brother who was under a long cross examination. Things got very personal while Firas Essa was on the stand. He told the jury that Yazeed gave his wife, Rosemarie, an STD after all the sexual partners that he had kept secret from her. Firas recalled how reckless his older brother was while he was married. He gave his wife and mistresses herpes.

programme.title: Sex, Cyanide And A Doctor On The Run Case
programme.desc: Fellow doctor and good friend of Yazeed Essa testified in his murder trial about the weeks following the death of his wife, Rosemarie. According to Dr. Sudesh Kedar, Yazeed told him about his infidelities and suggested that loneliness and going into hiding was best for him. This was just weeks before Yazeed fled the country. Jurors then learned about emails between the defendant and his good friend and colleague. The communication happened when Yazeed was living in exile after fleeing the U.S.

programme.title: Accomplice to Murder, With Vinnie Politan
programme.desc: Richard Kananen Sr. abused his wife and three children so badly that no one reported him missing.

programme.title: Accomplice to Murder, With Vinnie Politan
programme.desc: How quiet Meredith Moon became an accomplice to one of South Carolina's most horrific crimes.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: Scottsdale Spree Shooting follows a fast-paced investigation into a killing spree that claimed six victims.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: While jogging along a scenic canal, a young woman is brutally attacked and raped by a stranger. Police use groundbreaking genetic genealogy to identify the perpetrator.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Investigative genetic genealogy reveals the killer of a young mother murdered in her own home 34 years prior.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: When a beloved young woman is brutally murdered and left in an abandoned house in Brown County, Texas, investigative genetic genealogy could be the key to finding the perpetrator.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1992, the decapitated body of a young woman is discovered near the Arizona Canal in Phoenix. Ten months later, another body - this time a teen - is located nearby. Dedicated detectives use forensic science to catch the infamous killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Valentine's Day, 1974. Carla Walker is raped and killed, leading to a cold case that takes 46 years to crack.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Portageville, Missouri in 1998. The bodies of Megan and Sherri Scherer are found lifeless in their sitting room. Both had been shot in the head several times.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: This episode follows the exhaustive investigation into the murder of Tangie Simms and how genetic genealogy helped solve the crime.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: In 1982, 5-year-old Anne Pham vanishes on her way to kindergarten. Despite the FBI, the military police and the Seaside Police Department working together, the investigation goes cold until genealogy identifies her killer.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: Found shot in August 1986, Maria Del Pilar Delgadillo Carillo remains unidentified until February 2024.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace
programme.desc: A little girl's body is found, unidentified, in the Arizona desert in 1960. Across state lines, a family is reeling after their 4-year-old child is abducted. Sixty years later, science pieces it all together.

programme.desc: In 1978, the body of teen Krisann Baxter is found in Spokane, Washington. The case troubles investigators for over 40 years, but now a forensic technique may solve the mystery.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.desc: The NorCal Rapist terrorizes the women of North California for years until forensic science can identify him.

programme.desc: When Clare Holman is murdered in 1994, her case quickly goes cold, and many assume it will stay that way. But twenty years later, new technology finally provides answers.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives hosted by Nancy Grace

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A stranger snatched an 8-year-old girl as she played in her Tucson, Arizona neighborhood. Within hours, police began to suspect a drifter who was out on parole for abducting and molesting children.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Arizona, a shipment of money vanished without a trace, along with the two men hired to guard it. Agents didn't know if the guards were willing participants or victims of an ambush. Suspicions grew that the crime was an inside job.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1992, a young mother was gunned down in front of her children and police were left baffled. The crime seemed unmotivated and random. But family members hinted that the victim, who had been married to a prominent attorney, harbored a dark secret.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Six Black men had been brutally murdered; two had their hearts cut out. The only thing connecting the victims was their ethnicity and the fact that each had been shot with .22 caliber bullets.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves in the Alaskan wilderness. In one of the first cases of successful criminal investigative analysis, FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind and captured the serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Posing as a fashion photographer, serial killer Christopher Wilder (aka "The Beauty Queen Killer") lured young women from shopping malls with promises of fame and fortune. Most ended up dead.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1997, Jamie McMahan and Christopher Kauffman went on a crime spree of robbery, kidnapping and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A series of brutal bank robberies and murders led to an exhaustive eight-month investigation by the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.sub-title: The True Story of Mississippi Burning
programme.desc: In 1964, civil rights workers who set out to educate Southern Black people about the right to vote disappeared in Mississippi. After they were found lynched, the FBI gained the trust of an informant who led to the conviction of local KKK members.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Posing as a fashion photographer, serial killer Christopher Wilder (aka "The Beauty Queen Killer") lured young women from shopping malls with promises of fame and fortune. Most ended up dead.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1987, a television producer was gunned down in his New York office. Four years later and thousands of miles away, a man told Los Angeles police he had been hired to murder two Chippendale's dancers.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The bodies of several young women began turning up in shallow graves in the Alaskan wilderness. In one of the first cases of successful criminal investigative analysis, FBI profilers mapped the criminal's mind and captured the serial killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990, the body of a suspected drug courier was discovered burning in a Minnesota alleyway. The FBI teamed up with local police to uncover a dangerous crime ring run by a drug lord who used violence and intimidation to keep those around him quiet.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Drug gangs terrorized Harlem, New York neighborhoods for years in the early 1990s. After a 10-year-old boy became a crossfire victim, NYPD detectives and the FBI designed an elite task forced called C-11 to dismantle the gang.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1998, a wealthy woman disappeared from her home in Jackson, Mississippi. The only clue was a mysterious ransom note listing twelve names but no specific instructions. The FBI struggled to find answers in the cryptic message.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a van driver was murdered and the vehicle and its cargo of nearly a million dollars went missing.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A loner and distrustful of authority, Claude Dallas lived off the land, poaching wildlife and selling the hides. When two game wardens confronted Dallas about his deeds, they were shot and killed.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Joshua Wade had all the hall marks of a killer. We'll never understand the motives behind his random killings.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In March 2005, the gardaí recovered human body parts from the Royal Canal in Dublin. The victim's head was missing. He was identified as Farah Swaleh Noor, a Kenyan with a history of violence against women.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Robert Hansen was a monster who preyed on the most vulnerable women in society - dehumanising them for his own twisted fantasies. Stranded with their killer in the vast Alaskan wilderness his victims had no chance of survival.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Lonnie David Franklin Jr. is better known by the nickname 'Grim Sleeper.' He lured vulnerable women into his car before murdering and sexually assaulting them for more than two decades.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of a terrifying killer who snatched away the lives of four victims before being caught while trying to claim his fifth.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of Malcolm Green, who was jailed for life for the brutal murder of a Cardiff prostitute, only to kill again when he was released after 18 years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Gary Vinter, in prison for double murder, stabs a fellow inmate in the eye and gives another brain damage.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Rocky Beamon lures a woman into the woods and drowns her. Once in prison, he brutally kills two fellow inmates - and vows to kill again.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: The story of Tyler Davis Bingham, pretty criminal turned leader of the brutal and feared Aryan Brotherhood.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Denver Simmons is as dangerous as he is ruthless. Incarcerated for murdering a woman and her 14-year-old son, his violence only spiraled further once he was behind bars.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Prisoners
programme.desc: Feared L.A. crime boss Ezequiel Romo ruthlessly runs the streets of L.A. - even from behind bars. Leading the Blythe Street gang from his cell, he kills with a single phone call, coldly ordering the murders of anyone who stands in his way.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Cesar Barone was an American serial killer who took the life of four women in and around Hillsboro, Oregon in the 1990s.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2003, Kenneth Regan and William Horncy murdered a millionaire and his young family. The killers wiped out three generations of the Chohan family, including two young children.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A father took his children out for food after murdering his wife, then strangled them when they returned home.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: When Carol Jarvis disappeared, her husband told friends and family that she'd simply gone on a little holiday.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In 2007, single mother of three Paige Birgfeld went missing. Police in Grand Junction, Colorado investigating her disappearance discovered she'd been secretly working as an escort and suspected one of her clients of abducting and killing her.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2008, the body of Colorado real estate developer Alan Helmick was found by his wife, Miriam, who then became the prime suspect.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It was one of the most unusual cases in forensic history. Investigators had to find a way to solve a murder case with evidence which consisted of a squashed tomato found at the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Seattle police had no suspects in the violent murder of post-grunge singer, Mia Zapata. More than a decade passed before the evidence could be used by forensic scientists to identify the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The victim had ingested a massive amount of cyanide. An unlikely clue - a flaw on a mailing envelope - exposed a murderer who was willing to kill innocent people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: There was no clear reason for a young, healthy college student to be dead. But when the coroner discovered a tiny clue during the autopsy, investigators were able to uncover a mystery.

programme.title: Forensic Files

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 13-year-old girl went missing from her Colorado home and the only evidence the kidnapper left behind was three fingerprints on a window screen.

programme.desc: When a hit-and-run accident claimed the life of a high school athlete, everyone in town mourned his passing. Finding the killer was a long shot at best.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of an attractive young woman was found a mile from her abandoned car. Police were especially concerned when they realized the victim had come to them for protection just two weeks earlier.

programme.desc: The victim has been stabbed more than thirty times, and the crime scene is awash with her blood. Near her head, police discover a distinctive button with strands of thread still attached.
